parsed_review.summary: CP00-888 closes the RP29 Commercial Readiness P06 permission-matrix tail and opens the P07 failure-recovery foundation (RP29.P06.M06.S07-RP29.P07.M02.S17, 150 units, risk C) as descriptor-only evidence. The pack is correctly in pending_review with production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false; this receipt is the single hardened read-only review the manifest, adjudication, and construction-inspection are awaiting. Verified directly via Read/Grep: (1) section-row slices in registry.js align exactly with the 150 planned unit titles and the deliverable distribution sums to 150 and matches COMMERCIAL_CP888_REQUIREMENTS; (2) every permission and failure-recovery row sets runtime_execution/permission_decision_written/audit_event_written/runtime_receipt_emitted/real_client_data_included/credential_or_secret_included/recovery_action_executed/rollback/retry/compensation/cross_tenant_data_exposed to false, and these safe values are persisted in the stored commercial-readiness-contract.json (no safety flag is true anywhere); (3) authority_boundary keeps claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, and the Claude packet is read_only=true / promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, so Claude stays a read-only reviewer and not final approver; (4) the Hermes packet is review-evidence-only with emits_runtime_receipt=false, not product runtime; (5) validators.js enforces all boundaries with drift detection and model.test.js covers binding/coverage, the no-runtime bridge, and the on-disk contract; (6) auth preflight passed with valid JSON and no malformed/invalid receipt is being accepted as valid (invalid_review_attempts and valid_review_receipts empty). Unrelated dirty files (ldip, workbook, docx, progress-control-room) are correctly excluded from pack scope. The descriptor-verified production_ready claim is supported and the no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold. Two non-blocking P3 code-quality nits found. Provenance: source assertions are from my own inspection; command-evidence exit codes are pack-reported (Bash not run by me) and the full deterministic validation ladder remains to execute after review normalization.

findings.title: Dead no-op ternary expressions in failure-recovery row builder
findings.details: In createFailureRecoverySectionRows the fields 'failure_test_executes_runtime: title.includes("test") ? false : false' (line 1736) and 'failure_integration_smoke_executes_runtime: title === "Failure integration smoke" ? false : false' (line 1737) use conditionals whose both branches return false. The condition is therefore dead/confusing code. It is functionally safe because false is the correct no-runtime-execution value and validators.js:2097 independently asserts the flag is false, so the descriptor-only safety claim is unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Replace both ternaries with the literal value false (e.g., 'failure_test_executes_runtime: false') to remove the misleading conditional. Cosmetic only; does not block closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cleanup; safe as-is because the value is always the safe literal false and is enforced by the validator.

findings.title: Stored contract latest_projection rows not deep-equality-checked against generator
findings.details: validateCommercialReadinessContract validates the stored commercial-readiness-contract.json by checking top-level markers (schema_version, latest_pack.pack_id, latest_projection.descriptor name, validation.valid, mandatory_artifacts, no_leak_guards) and then runs the deep descriptor validation against a freshly generated descriptor (validateCommercialCp888PermissionFailureBridgeDescriptor called with default/undefined descriptor at line 2151), not against the stored contract.latest_projection row contents. The on-disk model.test.js case and validate script confirm the markers and the generated descriptor, but the persisted row bodies are not deep-compared to the generator output, so a hypothetical hand-edit of the stored JSON row bodies would not be caught as long as the markers match. This is the established pattern across the whole RP29 contract chain (CP873-CP888), not introduced by CP00-888, and I confirmed by grep that the stored JSON row safety flags are in fact all correct.
findings.recommendation: In a future hardening pass, add a deterministic deep-equality assertion between the stored contract.latest_projection (and projections.cp888) and the freshly generated descriptor so on-disk drift is detected. Pre-existing and out of this pack's introduced scope; non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational/pre-existing; non-blocking. Stored row values verified correct by direct grep for this pack.
